app.subtitle: Network mapping, made obvious — the full power of Nmap without the command line.
hint.file.new: Clear results and start a fresh target.
hint.file.save: Save the full result to reopen later.
hint.file.import: Load a target list from a .txt file (one per line).
hint.file.export_excel: Build a polished Excel report with Priorities, Hosts, Ports & NSE sheets.
hint.file.export_html: Self-contained HTML report to share or print.
hint.file.export_json: Raw data for automation or to reopen later.
hint.scan.start: Run the scan for the chosen target & profile.
hint.scan.nse: Pick deep-check scripts (vuln detection, enumeration, etc.).
hint.view.theme: Switch between dark and light appearance.
hint.view.learning: Show the Nmap flags & an explanation for each choice (great for learning).
hint.tools.nmap: See the Nmap engine version the app uses.
hint.tools.npcap: Check whether the Npcap driver (for SYN/UDP scans) is installed.
hint.help.licenses: Nmap (NPSL), Npcap, and this app's licenses.
hint.help.disclaimer: Terms of use & ethics — only scan what you're allowed to.
hint.target: Enter an IP, hostname, range, or CIDR. Separate multiple targets with spaces/commas.
hint.profile: Pick a ready-made scan profile. Hover to see what it does.
hint.command: Preview of the exact Nmap command that will run.
hint.custom_flags: Add raw Nmap flags, e.g. --max-retries 2 (for advanced users).
hint.tab.ports: Open ports with their service, version, and criticality.
hint.tab.details: Host details: OS, uptime, network distance, and criticality reasons.
hint.tab.nse: Raw output of the NSE scripts that were run.
hint.tab.priorities: What to look at first, sorted from most critical.
hint.tab.log: Live log of the scan process.
status.idle: Ready. Enter a target and press Start.
status.error: An error occurred during the scan.
log.no_nmap: Nmap not found. Install Nmap or use the full bundle.
banner.no_admin: Running without admin rights: using TCP Connect (-sT). For SYN/UDP/OS scans, run as Administrator.
banner.no_npcap: Npcap driver not installed. Some techniques (SYN/UDP/OS) need Npcap. TCP Connect still works.
banner.no_nmap: Nmap engine not found. See Help ▸ Quick Start to install it.
consent.title: Authorization Confirmation
consent.body: Only scan systems you own or have written permission to test. Are you authorized to scan this target?
consent.intrusive: This profile/script is intrusive and may disrupt the target's services. Continue only if you are fully authorized.
preset.quick.name: Quick Scan
preset.quick.hint: A fast look at the 100 most common open ports.
preset.quick_ver.name: Quick + Version
preset.intense.name: Intense Scan
preset.intense.hint: Full: ports, version detection, OS, default scripts, and traceroute.
preset.intense_all.name: Intense (All Ports)
preset.intense_all.hint: Like Intense, but scans all 65,535 ports (slow).
preset.intense_udp.name: Intense (UDP)
preset.intense_udp.hint: Includes UDP services like DNS & SNMP (slower).
preset.ping_sweep.name: Ping Sweep
preset.ping_sweep.hint: Map which hosts are up on a subnet, no port scan.
preset.discovery.name: Discovery
preset.discovery.hint: Thorough host discovery on a local network (ARP + probes).
preset.vuln.name: Vuln Scan
preset.vuln.hint: Find common vulnerabilities with the vuln NSE category (intrusive).
preset.stealth.name: Stealth/Slow
preset.stealth.hint: Slow & stealthy with fragmentation, for sensitive environments.
nse.cat.default: A safe, useful set of scripts run by -sC.
nse.cat.safe: Scripts that won't disrupt the target.
nse.cat.discovery: Dig deeper into the network & host.
nse.cat.external: Sends data to third-party services (privacy note).
nse.cat.fuzzer: Send malformed input to test robustness (intrusive).
nse.cat.dos: Denial-of-service tests — CAN DAMAGE the target.
nse.bundle.common_vuln: Check common vulnerabilities across all open services.
nse.bundle.smb_enum: Windows info, share listing, & EternalBlue (MS17-010) check.
nse.bundle.http_enum: Title, headers, folders, & methods on web services.
nse.bundle.dns_checks: Check open DNS recursion & zone transfer (AXFR).
nse.bundle.cve_corr: Correlate service versions with the CVE database (vulners).
nse.title: Choose NSE Scripts
nse.intro: NSE is Nmap's 'super power' for deep checks. Pick a ready bundle or category. Hover for an explanation.
crit.rdp: Remote Desktop (RDP) — a ransomware favourite when exposed to the internet.
crit.smb: Windows file sharing (SMB) — source of EternalBlue/WannaCry if outdated.
crit.telnet: Telnet — unencrypted admin access; credentials are sent in the clear.
crit.vnc: VNC — remote desktop often left without a password.
crit.rservices: Legacy remote services (rlogin/rsh) that are insecure.
crit.ftp: FTP — cleartext file transfer; check for anonymous login.
crit.tftp: TFTP — no authentication, often leaks configs.
crit.ssh: SSH — safe when well configured; still a brute-force target.
crit.db: Database — must not be exposed directly to the internet.
crit.nosql: NoSQL database — classic data breach when left without auth.
crit.memcached: Memcached — no auth; DDoS amplification risk.
crit.docker: Unauthenticated Docker API = full host takeover.
crit.http: Web (HTTP) — risk depends on the app behind it.
crit.httpalt: Alternate web port — often admin panels / internal apps.
crit.proxy: Proxy — can be abused as an open proxy if exposed.
crit.smtp: SMTP — an open relay can be abused for spam.
crit.dns: DNS — can be used for amplification or zone leaks if misconfigured.
crit.snmp: SNMP — default community strings leak device info.
crit.ldap: LDAP — anonymous bind can leak directory data.
crit.ntp: NTP — the monlist feature risks DDoS amplification.
crit.rpc: RPC — a common starting point for Windows/Unix enumeration.
crit.nfs: NFS — Unix file shares that are often world-readable.
crit.x11: X11 — often unauthenticated; can capture screen/keystrokes.
crit.ics: Industrial protocols (OT/ICS) — exposure can have physical impact.
crit.upnp: UPnP — can leak internal device information.
crit.generic: Open port — check whether this service really needs to be exposed.
crit.f.public: Exposed to the internet (public) — much higher risk.
crit.f.cleartext: Unencrypted protocol — data/credentials can be sniffed.
crit.f.noauth: Appears to allow access without authentication / anonymously.
crit.f.kev: This vulnerability is known to be actively exploited — fix urgently.
crit.advice.database: Restrict to trusted networks, require strong auth, never expose to the internet.
crit.advice.remote: Limit access sources (firewall/VPN), enable MFA, disable if unused.
crit.advice.fileshare: Close public access, apply least-privilege permissions, disable legacy protocols (SMBv1).
crit.advice.generic: Review whether this service needs to be open; close it if not required.
quickstart.body:
1) Type a target (e.g. example.com or 192.168.1.0/24).
2) Pick a profile — start with Quick Scan.
3) Press Start, then open the Priorities tab to see what needs attention.

Hover over any menu/option to see its explanation in the status bar.
